recurringPattern: 3x2 or 3x3 grid of feature cards each containing an icon in a colored circle/square, a bold short headline, and two lines of body copy, all with identical border-radius and subtle shadows.
designHarm: Features become wallpaper; the uniform card grid treats every feature as equally important with no visual hierarchy. Users cannot distinguish the primary value proposition from secondary niceties.
legitimateExceptions: Presentation sites where every feature is genuinely co-equal
Documentation overview sections that need scannable category tiles
possibleFixtures: Feature grid with intentionally varied card sizes to signal hierarchy
Alternate layout using staggered rows or feature-list format
originalObservations: AI-generated feature sections consistently default to a uniform-card grid regardless of the number or priority of features.
The icon containers are always the same size and shape (usually a 48px circle with a 24px icon inside).
Card copy is always bold headline + exactly two lines of body text.
